name: Network Monitoring & SON
description: Sub-sector template for network monitoring, self-organizing network (SON), and network management patents. Covers performance monitoring, fault detection, automatic parameter optimization, self-healing, and RAN intelligent controller (RIC) functions.
contextDescription: SON and network monitoring automate the operation of cellular networks. Every operator uses SON functions for mobility optimization, coverage and capacity optimization, and fault detection. The O-RAN RIC (RAN Intelligent Controller) is the modern implementation of SON principles. Key targets: SON vendors (TEOCO, Amdocs, Cellwize), O-RAN xApp/rApp developers, and infrastructure vendors. AI/ML-driven SON is the emerging paradigm. Per-deployment value is high — SON reduces operational costs for carriers.
scoringGuidance: Every cellular operator uses SON — it's essential for managing thousands of cells.
O-RAN RIC (xApps/rApps) is the modern evolution of SON — growing rapidly.
AI/ML-driven network optimization patents are highest-value in this space.
Self-healing (automatic fault detection and recovery) reduces downtime — high operator value.
Mobility optimization (handover parameter tuning) affects every connected device.

questions.question: What SON function does this patent address? Score 8-10 for AI/ML-driven SON or O-RAN RIC xApp/rApp, 5-7 for specific SON functions (MRO, MLB, CCO), 3-4 for basic network parameter optimization, 1-2 for manual network management.
questions.reasoningPrompt: AI/ML-driven SON and O-RAN RIC are the highest-value areas.

questions.question: Does this patent address automatic fault detection, diagnosis, or recovery (self-healing)? Score 8-10 for autonomous fault recovery without human intervention, 5-7 for automatic fault detection with suggested actions, 3-4 for monitoring and alerting, 1-2 for no self-healing aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Self-healing reduces OpEx significantly — operators value autonomous recovery.

questions.question: Does this patent align with O-RAN RIC architecture (near-RT RIC, non-RT RIC, xApp, rApp)? Score 8-10 for direct RIC function implementation, 5-7 for RIC-compatible concepts, 3-4 for general network intelligence, 1-2 for no RIC relevance.
questions.reasoningPrompt: O-RAN RIC is the standard platform for network intelligence — alignment means broad licensing.

questions.question: Does this patent address UE measurement reporting, network KPI collection, or MDT (minimization of drive tests)? Score 8-10 for novel measurement techniques or MDT innovations, 5-7 for KPI aggregation and analytics, 3-4 for basic measurement reporting, 1-2 for no measurement aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: MDT and UE measurements are standardized in 3GPP — exercised by every device.

questions.question: How detectable is this SON/monitoring innovation? Score 8-10 for observable in network behavior (parameter changes, KPI improvements), 5-7 for detectable in SON platform configuration, 3-4 for internal algorithm, 1-2 for theoretical only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: SON effects are observable — network parameters that auto-optimize can be monitored.

questions.question: How significantly does this patent reduce operator operational expenses? Score 8-10 for major OpEx reduction (>20% for specific operations), 5-7 for moderate improvement, 3-4 for marginal efficiency gain, 1-2 for no clear OpEx impact.
questions.reasoningPrompt: OpEx reduction is the primary value driver for SON — quantify the impact.
